content.text:
A normal subgroup is one that is "well-centered" within its parent 
group—invariant under conjugation by any group element. This property 
is essential for constructing quotient groups.

content.title: Normal Subgroup
content.content:
A subgroup $N$ of $G$ is **normal** in $G$, 
written $N \trianglelefteq G$, if for all $g \in G$ and $n \in N$:
\[
 gng^{-1} \in N
\]
Equivalently, $gNg^{-1} = N$ for all $g \in G$.

content.title: Equivalent Conditions for Normality
content.content:
For a subgroup $N \leq G$, the following are equivalent:

1. $N \trianglelefteq G$

2. $gN = Ng$ for all $g \in G$ (left cosets equal right cosets)

3. $gNg^{-1} \subseteq N$ for all $g \in G$

4. $N$ is a union of conjugacy classes of $G$
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
We prove (i) $\Leftrightarrow$ (ii).

$(\text{i}) \Rightarrow (\text{ii})$: If $gn \in gN$, then 
$gn = (gng^{-1})g \in Ng$ since $gng^{-1} \in N$. Thus $gN \subseteq Ng$. 
Similarly, $Ng \subseteq gN$.

$(\text{ii}) \Rightarrow (\text{i})$: If $gN = Ng$, then $gng^{-1} \in 
gNg^{-1} = Ngg^{-1} = N$.

content.title: Normal subgroups in abelian groups
content.problem:
In any abelian group $G$, every subgroup is normal, since 
$gng^{-1} = gg^{-1}n = n \in N$ for all $n \in N$.

content.title: The alternating group $A_n \trianglelefteq S_n$
content.problem:
$A_n$ is normal in $S_n$ because it has index $2$. Alternatively, 
$\sigma A_n \sigma^{-1} = A_n$ for all $\sigma \in S_n$ because 
conjugation preserves parity.

content.title: Center of a Group
content.content:
The **center** of $G$ is
\[
 Z(G) = \{z \in G : zg = gz \text{ for all } g \in G\}
\]
For $z \in Z(G)$ and $g \in G$: $gzg^{-1} = zgg^{-1} = z \in Z(G)$.

content.title: Commutator Subgroup
content.content:
The **commutator subgroup** (or derived 
subgroup) of $G$ is
\[
 G' = [G, G] = \langle aba^{-1}b^{-1} : a, b \in G \rangle
\]

items:
Prove that if $N \trianglelefteq G$ and $H \leq G$, then 
 $N \cap H \trianglelefteq H$.
Find the center of $D_4$ (the dihedral group of order $8$).

Show that the only normal subgroups of $S_3$ are $\{e\}$, 
 $A_3$, and $S_3$.

marginNotes.text:
Great peace have those who love your 
law, and nothing can make them stumble.
